title: InputData
description: A Mapbox-powered geospatial visualization widget for displaying location-based data on interactive maps. Use this widget for GPS tracking, asset locations, geographic heatmaps, route visualization, or any data with latitude/longitude coordinates. Supports multiple layer types including circles (points), symbols (icons), heatmaps (density), and lines (tracks/routes). Features auto-follow mode to keep markers in view, multiple map styles, and pivot-based auto-generation of layers from data columns. Ideal for fleet management, IoT device monitoring, field service tracking, and geographic data analysis.

title.title: Title
title.description: The main heading displayed above the map. Use to describe what geographic data is being shown (e.g., 'Fleet Locations', 'Sensor Network').

subTitle.title: Subtitle
subTitle.description: Secondary text displayed below the title. Use for additional context like region, time period, or data source.

style.title: Map Style
style.description: The visual appearance of the base map: 'streets' for road-focused, 'outdoors' for terrain, 'light/dark' for minimal designs, 'satellite' for aerial imagery, 'navigation' for routing focus.

showLegend.title: Show Legend
showLegend.description: When enabled, displays a legend identifying each data layer by color and label. Useful for multi-layer maps.

follow.title: Auto Follow
follow.description: When enabled, the map automatically pans and zooms to keep all data points in view as they update. Disable for fixed-view maps where users control the viewport.

dataseries.title: Dataseries
dataseries.description: Array of map layers. Each layer displays geographic data points with its own visualization style (circles, symbols, heatmap, or lines).

label.title: Series Label
label.description: Display name for this map layer shown in legends and tooltips. Should identify what entities or data this layer represents.

type.title: Layer type
type.description: Visualization style: 'circle' for point markers, 'symbol' for icon markers with labels, 'heatmap' for density visualization, 'line' for connected paths/routes.

color.title: Layer Base Color
color.description: Primary color for this layer's markers or fill. Used as default when data-driven coloring isn't configured.

latestValues.title: Latest Values
latestValues.description: Limit displayed data to the N most recent points per pivot group. Useful for showing only current positions without historical trail. Leave empty to show all data.

circleConfig.title: Circle Layer Configuration
circleConfig.description: Styling options specific to circle (point) layers.

circle-blur.title: Blur
circle-blur.description: Edge blur effect from 0 (sharp edges) to 1 (fully blurred from center). Use slight blur (0.1-0.3) for softer appearance.

circle-opacity.title: Opacity
circle-opacity.description: Circle transparency from 0 (invisible) to 1 (fully opaque). Use lower values when circles overlap to see density.

text-color.title: Text Color
text-color.description: Color of label text displayed on or near the circle markers.

text-size.title: Text Size
text-size.description: Font size in pixels for label text on circle markers.

symbolConfig.title: Symbol Layer Configuration
symbolConfig.description: Styling options specific to symbol (icon) layers.

text-color.title: Text Color
text-color.description: Color of label text displayed near the symbol icons.

text-size.title: Text Size
text-size.description: Font size in pixels for label text on symbol markers.

icon-image.title: Icon Name
icon-image.description: Icon identifier from available icons: 'marker' for location pin, 'car-front'/'car-top' for vehicle tracking. See Maki icon set at https://labs.mapbox.com/maki-icons/ for more options.

icon-size.title: Icon size
icon-size.description: Scale multiplier for the icon (1 = original size, 2 = double size, 0.5 = half size).

heatmapConfig.title: Heatmap Layer Configuration
heatmapConfig.description: Styling options specific to heatmap (density) layers.

heatmap-intensity.title: Intensity
heatmap-intensity.description: Heat intensity multiplier. Higher values make the heatmap appear 'hotter' with colors reaching maximum faster. Default is 1.

heatmap-opacity.title: Opacity
heatmap-opacity.description: Heatmap layer transparency from 0 (invisible) to 1 (fully opaque). Lower values let the base map show through.

heatmap-radius.title: Radius Base Size
heatmap-radius.description: Base radius in pixels for each point's heat contribution. Combined with data value to determine actual heat spread. Larger radius = more blending.

lineConfig.title: Track Layer Configuration
lineConfig.description: Styling options specific to line (track/route) layers.

line-width.title: Line Width
line-width.description: Thickness of the route line in pixels. Use thicker lines for emphasis or when zoomed out.

icon-image.title: Icon Name
icon-image.description: Icon displayed at the current position (end of line): 'marker' for generic location, 'car-front'/'car-top' for vehicles.

icon-size.title: Icon size
icon-size.description: Scale multiplier for the position icon (1 = original size).

data.title: Map data
data.description: Array of geographic data points. Each point must have latitude and longitude coordinates, with optional altitude, value, and pivot for grouping.

lon.title: Longitude
lon.description: The east-west coordinate in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Example: -122.4194 for San Francisco.

lat.title: Latitude
lat.description: The north-south coordinate in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Example: 37.7749 for San Francisco.

alt.title: Altitude
alt.description: Optional elevation/altitude value in meters. Can be used for 3D visualization or displayed in tooltips.

value.title: Data point value
value.description: Numeric value associated with this location. Used for heatmap intensity, marker sizing, or tooltip display.

pivot.title: Split Data by
pivot.description: Column value used to split data into multiple layers automatically. Each unique pivot value creates a separate map layer (e.g., pivot by 'vehicle_id' for fleet tracking).
